description: Divergence ledger (Track B). Catalogs places where the DIF DWN prose spec is wrong, silent, or an explicit TODO, and the implementation is the de-facto truth. This is an upstream-contribution backlog, NOT a conformance test. The reference being diverged from is the DIF DWN spec named in source.spec; each entry records the impl behavior, what the spec says (or fails to say), and a disposition.

spec.name: Decentralized Web Node (DWN)
spec.section: Record ID Generation, Context ID Generation

impl.behavior: recordId = CIDv1(dag-cbor({ ...descriptor, author })). The author DID is folded into the CID input alongside the descriptor fields.

spec.says: The prose Record ID Generation algorithm computes recordId = CID({ descriptorCid }) — a CID over an object containing only the descriptorCid, with NO author DID. This omits the author and does not match any known interoperable implementation.

proof.author: did:example:alice
proof.note: implRecordId = CID({ ...descriptor, author }); specRecordId = CID({ descriptorCid }). The two literals were derived from an independent pure-Python DAG-CBOR encoder and must remain distinct while this divergence holds.

impl.behavior: entryId is defined as the recordId derivation: CIDv1(dag-cbor({ ...descriptor, author })). An initial write is detected by entryId == recordId.

spec.says: The prose spec never defines entryId as an algorithm. The term is used implicitly in surrounding machinery but no Entry ID Generation section exists.

proof.note: No spec-prose algorithm exists for entryId, so there is nothing to recompute and contrast. Documentation-only ledger entry: the impl is the de-facto definition.

impl.behavior: contextId = parentContextId + "/" + entryId; the root contextId equals the recordId. Integrity validation enforces that a parent-less write has contextId == recordId.

spec.says: The Context ID Generation section of the prose spec is an empty TODO ("::: todo :::"). No algorithm is provided.

proof.note: The spec section is an empty TODO, so there is no prose algorithm to recompute. Documentation-only ledger entry: the impl is the de-facto definition.
